rationale: Wrap the body in `if H { ... }` to materialise the two ensures-instantiations as body-level hypotheses, then in the (ok1 && ok2) case close the goal directly: re-assert each `forall |i|` for both runs, do an `assert forall|i| ne1[i] == ne2[i] by { ... }` that re-instantiates `send_log_entry_reflects_packet` on both runs against the shared `cpackets@[i]`, lift to whole-sequence equality with `=~=`, derive `post1_netc.history() == post2_netc.history()` from the shared offset, and finally close with `assert(post1_netc.view() == post2_netc.view())` and `assert(det_send_packet_seq_equal(r1, r2, post1_netc, post2_netc))` so z3 has an explicit named goal to chain the field-level equalities onto.
